mathematical_rationale: The CutAndPaste property states that if two input pairs $(x_1, y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2)$ produce identical crossing sequences, then the output remains unchanged when swapping the $Y$-components: $\text{Out}(x_1, y_1) = \text{Out}(x_1, y_2)$. For a fooling set $S$, we have $\text{Out}(x_i, y_i) = \text{true}$ and for $x_1 \neq x_2$ either $\text{Out}(x_1, y_2) = \text{false}$ or $\text{Out}(x_2, y_1) = \text{false}$. Suppose distinct pairs $p_1, p_2 \in S$ with $p_1 \neq p_2$ have the same crossing sequence. Then by CutAndPaste we could swap $Y$-components and preserve the output, contradicting the fooling set property. Therefore $\text{CS}$ must be injective on $S$.
lean_4_code_delta:
import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic

variable {X Y : Type} [DecidableEq X] [DecidableEq Y] [DecidableEq (X × Y)]

-- Re-stating FoolingSet definition for completeness within this delta.
def FoolingSet (Out : X × Y → Bool) (S : Finset (X × Y)) : Prop :=
  (∀ p ∈ S, Out p = true) /\
  (∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, p1.1 ≠ p2.1 → Out (p1.1, p2.2) = false \/ Out (p2.1, p1.2) = false)

variable {CrossSeq : Type} (CS : X × Y → CrossSeq) (Out : X × Y → Bool)

def CutAndPaste : Prop :=
  ∀ x1 y1 x2 y2, CS (x1, y1) = CS (x2, y2) → Out (x1, y1) = Out (x1, y2)

theorem fooling_set_implies_CS_injective_on_S (S : Finset (X × Y))
    (h_fool : FoolingSet Out S) (h_cut : CutAndPaste CS Out) :
    ∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, CS p1 = CS p2 → p1 = p2 := by
  intro p1 hp1 p2 hp2 hCS
  by_contra h_neq
  have h_out_true1 := h_fool.1 p1 hp1
  have h_out_true2 := h_fool.1 p2 hp2
  have h_xy_neq : p1.1 ≠ p2.1 :=
    if h : p1.1 = p2.1 then
      have : p1.2 ≠ p2.2 := fun h' => h_neq (Prod.ext h h')
      have h_false_cond := h_fool.2 p1 hp1 p2 hp2 (by simp [h])
      cases h_false_cond with
      | inl hf =>
          rw [h_cut (p1.1, p1.2) (p1.1, p2.2) p1 p2] at h_out_true1
          · rw [hf] at h_out_true1; contradiction
          · exact hCS
      | inr hf =>
          rw [h_cut (p2.1, p2.2) (p2.1, p1.2) p2 p1] at h_out_true2
          · rw [hf] at h_out_true2; contradiction
          · rw [hCS]
    else h
  have h_false_cond := h_fool.2 p1 hp1 p2 hp2 h_xy_neq
  cases h_false_cond with
  | inl hf =>
      rw [h_cut (p1.1, p1.2) (p1.1, p2.2) p1 p2] at h_out_true1
      · rw [hf] at h_out_true1; contradiction
      · exact hCS
  | inr hf =>
      rw [h_cut (p2.1, p2.2) (p2.1, p1.2) p2 p1] at h_out_true2
      · rw [hf] at h_out_true2; contradiction
      · rw [hCS]
expected_verifier_state: Definitions `FoolingSet` and `CutAndPaste` added; theorem `fooling_set_implies_CS_injective_on_S` proven, establishing that under the CutAndPaste property the crossing sequence map CS is injective on any fooling set S.
